rulings.Case Title: FLAVIA PINTO VS. KELVIN YEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 9, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF FLAVIA PINTO MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Counsel to prepare an order. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: J. DENNIS BONNEY ET AL VS. 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Defendant 1810 Jackson Street Homeowners Association
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 9, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF J. DENNIS BONNEY AN INDIVIDUAL, AND J. DENNIS BONNEY, TRUSTEE, VIVIAN KENNEDY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST DEFENDANT 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION is DENIED. There is no evidence provided to support the requested 3-part injunction. Plaintiff presents no evidence that the Defendant is in the process or planning any actions as to any of the requests. To the extent, Plaintiff is seeking some type of a mandatory injunction pertaining to already recorded document, Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that such relief may be granted on a motion. Furthermore, Plaintiff presents no evidence of any irreparable harm that cannot be compensated by money-damages should the Plaintiff prevail in this action. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BROKERS CONNECTION, INC. VS. XIAO LING LIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 9, 2024 line 10. DEFENDANT XIAO LIN MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is GRANTED, subject to (1) defendant remitting to Plaintiff the sum of $2,141.20 representing Plaintiff's costs and obtaining the default and the writ, and the daily damages associated with delay in seeking the relief. The Court notes that this delay and Plaintiff's costs could have been avoided if Defendant served the Answer on the Plaintiff at the time Defendant purportedly attempted to file the Answer and if Defendant sought relieve promptly after finding out about the default; (2) filing an answer no later than 4:00 p.m. on December 9, 2024 after remitting the payment to the Plaintiff. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
